articles.headline: Nationwide Protests Erupt Against UGC Bill 2026, Education Sector Demands Review
articles.explainer:
• Students, faculty, and education activists across India staged massive protests against the proposed UGC Bill 2026, citing concerns over autonomy and access.
• Key contentious provisions include increased private sector participation, reduced government funding, and centralisation of regulatory powers.
• Critics argue the Bill could lead to commercialisation of higher education, making it inaccessible for economically weaker sections.
• The government defends the Bill, stating it aims to enhance quality, efficiency, and global competitiveness of Indian universities.
• Demonstrations were particularly strong in major educational hubs, with calls for a parliamentary review and wider stakeholder consultation.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: UGC Bill 2026 proposes major changes to higher education regulation.
Concerns include funding, private sector role, and institutional autonomy.
UGC (University Grants Commission) is a statutory body under Ministry of Education.
Protests highlight issues of access and equity in education.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the potential impact of the UGC Bill 2026 on the accessibility, quality, and equity of higher education in India.
Discuss the role of government in regulating higher education and balancing autonomy with accountability.
Evaluate the arguments for and against increased private sector involvement in education.
Suggest measures to ensure inclusive and quality education while pursuing reforms.
articles.imageDescription: Students protesting with banners outside a university campus, demanding withdrawal of the UGC Bill 2026.
articles.trivia: The University Grants Commission (UGC) was established in 1956, though its origins can be traced back to the recommendations of the Radhakrishnan Commission in 1948.

articles.headline: India Showcases 'Nari Shakti' and Indigenous Defence Prowess at 77th Republic Day Parade
articles.explainer:
• The 77th Republic Day Parade on Kartavya Path highlighted 'Nari Shakti' (women power) with all-women contingents and performances.
• Indigenous defence capabilities were prominently displayed, including advanced missile systems, tanks, and newly inducted drones.
• President Droupadi Murmu addressed the nation, emphasizing unity, progress, and the spirit of 'Viksit Bharat' by 2047.
• French President Emmanuel Macron graced the occasion as the Chief Guest, strengthening Indo-French strategic partnership.
• Cultural tableaux from various states showcased India's rich diversity and developmental initiatives, drawing widespread acclaim.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution
Significant provisions of the Indian Constitution
Government Policies and Interventions for Development
India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Republic Day is celebrated on January 26 to commemorate the adoption of the Constitution.
French President Emmanuel Macron was the Chief Guest for 2026.
Theme of the parade focused on 'Nari Shakti' and 'Viksit Bharat'.
Kartavya Path (formerly Rajpath) is the venue for the Republic Day Parade.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of Republic Day celebrations in reinforcing national identity and constitutional values.
Discuss how the emphasis on 'Nari Shakti' reflects India's progress in gender equality and women empowerment.
Evaluate the role of such national events in showcasing India's defence capabilities and promoting 'Make in India' initiatives.
Examine the geopolitical implications of inviting key foreign leaders as Republic Day Chief Guests, with specific reference to India-France relations.
articles.imageDescription: An all-women contingent marching proudly down Kartavya Path during the Republic Day Parade.
articles.trivia: The first Republic Day Parade was held in 1950 at Rajpath, which was renamed Kartavya Path in 2022.

articles.headline: Economic Survey 2025-26 Hints at Robust Growth, Focus on Green Economy & Digital Public Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Economic Survey 2025-26, released ahead of the Union Budget, projects India's economy to maintain a strong growth trajectory.
• It emphasizes the crucial role of green economy initiatives and Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in driving sustainable development.
• The survey highlights continued capital expenditure by the government and private sector investment as key growth drivers.
• Challenges identified include global supply chain disruptions, inflationary pressures, and the need for skill development.
• Recommendations include further reforms in agriculture, labour, and financial sectors to unlock full potential.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Government Budgeting
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Economic Survey is released annually by the Ministry of Finance before the Union Budget.
It provides a detailed review of the economy's performance.
Key focus areas for 2025-26 survey include green economy and DPI.
Projections for GDP growth are usually provided.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key findings of the Economic Survey 2025-26 regarding India's growth prospects and challenges.
Discuss the significance of 'Green Economy' and 'Digital Public Infrastructure' in achieving sustainable and inclusive growth.
Evaluate the government's strategy of increasing capital expenditure to stimulate economic activity.
Suggest policy measures to address the identified challenges of inflation and global supply chain disruptions.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic illustrating India's projected economic growth trajectory with pillars representing green economy and digital infrastructure.
articles.trivia: The first Economic Survey of India was presented in 1950-51, and until 1964, it was presented along with the Union Budget.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Economic Survey and Key Economic Indicators

articles.headline: India-France Elevate Strategic Dialogue Post Republic Day, Focus on Defence & Space Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• Following the Republic Day celebrations, India and France held high-level strategic talks between NSA Ajit Doval and his French counterpart.
• Discussions centered on deepening defence cooperation, including technology transfer, joint production, and maritime security in the Indo-Pacific.
• Space collaboration, particularly in Earth observation and satellite navigation, emerged as a key area for future partnership.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to multilateralism and addressing global challenges like terrorism and climate change.
• The dialogue reinforced the 'Horizon 2047' roadmap for the bilateral relationship, aiming for enhanced strategic autonomy.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
India and its neighbourhood- relations
prelimsPoints: India and France are strategic partners, with cooperation in defence, space, and civil nuclear energy.
French President was Chief Guest at 2026 Republic Day.
Discussions focused on defence procurement, joint ventures, and space applications.
Horizon 2047 roadmap guides bilateral relations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving dynamics of India-France strategic partnership, particularly in the context of Indo-Pacific security.
Discuss the significance of defence technology transfer and joint production in enhancing India's strategic autonomy.
Evaluate the potential for expanded space cooperation between India and France and its implications for both nations.
Examine how bilateral ties contribute to addressing global challenges and promoting a multipolar world order.
articles.imageDescription: National Security Advisors of India and France shaking hands during a high-level meeting.
articles.trivia: France was the first country to sign a Strategic Partnership with India in 1998.

articles.headline: ISRO's Shukrayaan-2 Mission Gears Up for Launch, Focus on Venusian Atmosphere & Surface Mapping
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) provided an update on the Shukrayaan-2 mission, confirming its readiness for an upcoming launch window.
• The mission aims to study the Venusian atmosphere, its composition, and potential geological activity using advanced instruments.
• Key objectives include high-resolution mapping of Venus's surface and subsurface features, utilizing synthetic aperture radar (SAR).
• Shukrayaan-2 builds upon global efforts to understand Venus, often called Earth's 'evil twin' due to its extreme conditions.
• The mission underscores India's growing capabilities in interplanetary exploration and scientific research.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Shukrayaan-2 is ISRO's proposed mission to Venus.
Objectives include studying Venus's atmosphere and surface.
Utilizes Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) for mapping.
It is part of India's interplanetary exploration program.
mainsPoints: Discuss the scientific objectives and potential contributions of ISRO's Shukrayaan-2 mission to planetary science.
Evaluate India's growing capabilities in space exploration and its implications for global scientific collaboration.
Examine the technological challenges involved in missions to Venus and how ISRO is addressing them.
Highlight the significance of such missions in inspiring scientific temper and technological innovation within the country.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of the Shukrayaan-2 orbiter approaching Venus, with scientific instruments deployed.
articles.trivia: Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system, despite being farther from the Sun than Mercury, due to its dense atmosphere causing a runaway greenhouse effect.

articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Achieves Key Milestones, Boosts Renewable Energy Sector
articles.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission announced significant progress in establishing green hydrogen production facilities and infrastructure.
• Several pilot projects for green hydrogen applications in steel, refining, and transportation sectors have commenced operations.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Government incentives and policy support are attracting substantial private investment into the renewable energy sector.
• This advancement is crucial for India's climate commitments and achieving energy independence by 2047.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to establish India as a global green hydrogen hub.
Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy sources via electrolysis.
Applications include steel, refining, and transportation.
It is crucial for India's climate goals and energy security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of transitioning to a green hydrogen-based economy.
Examine the challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and infrastructure in India.
Suggest policy interventions and technological advancements required to accelerate the adoption of green hydrogen across sectors.
articles.imageDescription: An industrial facility with electrolyzers producing green hydrogen, powered by wind turbines in the background.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, making it a zero-carbon fuel.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Core Tenets of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, Emphasizes Right to Privacy
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a significant ruling today, largely upholding the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023.
• The court emphasized the fundamental right to privacy as enshrined in Article 21 and its careful balance with legitimate state interests.
• Specific provisions related to data fiduciary obligations, data principal rights, and cross-border data transfers were clarified.
• The ruling provides much-needed legal clarity for technology companies and data processors operating in India.
• Critics' concerns regarding government exemptions and potential surveillance were partially addressed, with calls for strict oversight mechanisms.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: DPDP Act 2023 regulates personal data processing in India.
Supreme Court upheld its constitutional validity.
Right to Privacy is a fundamental right under Article 21.
The Act defines data fiduciary, data principal, and their rights/obligations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the Supreme Court's rationale in upholding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act and its implications for individual privacy in the digital age.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities presented by the DPDP Act for businesses and the government in managing personal data.
Evaluate the balance struck by the Act between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests, including national security.
Suggest mechanisms to ensure effective implementation and prevent misuse of exemptions under the DPDP Act.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal documents, symbolizing a Supreme Court judgment on data protection.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) by the Supreme Court declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Saksham Nari' Initiative to Boost Women's Entrepreneurship and Digital Literacy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched 'Saksham Nari', a pan-India initiative aimed at empowering women.
• The program focuses on promoting women's entrepreneurship through skill development, financial assistance, and market linkages.
• A significant component includes enhancing digital literacy among women in rural and semi-urban areas to bridge the digital divide.
• Partnerships with self-help groups (SHGs), NGOs, and private sector entities are central to the initiative's implementation.
• 'Saksham Nari' seeks to foster economic independence and social inclusion for women, aligning with 'Viksit Bharat' goals.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization
Population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues
Social empowerment
articles.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: 'Saksham Nari' is a government initiative for women empowerment.
Focuses on entrepreneurship, skill development, and digital literacy.
Implemented by Ministry of Women and Child Development.
Aims to involve SHGs and private sector for market linkages.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted approach of the 'Saksham Nari' initiative in promoting women's empowerment and its potential impact on gender equality.
Analyze how enhancing digital literacy among women can bridge the digital divide and foster economic independence.
Evaluate the role of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and public-private partnerships in the successful implementation of such social welfare programs.
Suggest additional measures to further support women's entrepreneurship and ensure sustainable livelihoods for women in India.
articles.imageDescription: A group of women in a rural setting attending a digital literacy workshop, using tablets.
articles.trivia: The 'Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao' campaign, launched in 2015, aims to address the declining Child Sex Ratio and empower girls in India.

articles.headline: India Accelerates Border Infrastructure Development in Eastern Sector Amid Geopolitical Shifts
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced accelerated projects for road, bridge, and tunnel construction along India's eastern borders.
• The push for infrastructure development aims to enhance troop mobility, logistics, and surveillance capabilities in strategically critical regions.
• Modernization includes advanced surveillance technologies and integration with local communities for intelligence gathering.
• The move is seen as a response to evolving geopolitical dynamics and security challenges in the Himalayan frontiers.
• Local employment generation and improved connectivity for border populations are additional benefits cited by officials.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
prelimsPoints: India is accelerating infrastructure development on its eastern borders.
Projects include roads, bridges, and tunnels.
Aims to enhance troop mobility and surveillance.
Response to geopolitical shifts and security challenges.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic imperatives behind India's accelerated border infrastructure development in the eastern sector.
Analyze how enhanced connectivity and surveillance capabilities contribute to national security and border management.
Evaluate the challenges faced in constructing infrastructure in difficult Himalayan terrain and the technological solutions employed.
Examine the socio-economic impact of such projects on border populations and their role in national integration.
articles.imageDescription: Construction workers building a new road through rugged mountainous terrain near an international border.
articles.trivia: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is responsible for building and maintaining road networks in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries.

articles.headline: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme Expanded to New Sectors, Boosts Manufacturing and Exports
articles.explainer:
• The government announced the expansion of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme to cover additional strategic sectors.
• New sectors include advanced textiles, specific medical devices, and components for electric vehicles, alongside existing ones.
• The expansion aims to further boost domestic manufacturing, attract foreign investment, and enhance India's export competitiveness.
• The scheme provides incentives on incremental sales from products manufactured in India, encouraging scale and efficiency.
• Industry experts commend the move as a catalyst for job creation and integration into global supply chains.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
articles.source: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme offers incentives on incremental sales for products manufactured in India.
Expanded to new sectors like advanced textiles and EV components.
Aims to boost manufacturing, attract investment, and increase exports.
Managed by various ministries and departments.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and effectiveness of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme in transforming India's manufacturing sector.
Discuss the rationale behind expanding the PLI scheme to new sectors and its potential impact on industrial growth and employment.
Evaluate the role of the PLI scheme in attracting foreign investment and integrating India into global supply chains.
Examine the challenges in implementation and suggest measures to maximize the scheme's benefits for sustainable economic development.
articles.imageDescription: A factory floor with advanced machinery, symbolizing increased domestic manufacturing under the PLI scheme.
articles.trivia: The PLI scheme was first introduced in March 2020 for three sectors and later expanded to 14 key sectors.

articles.headline: India Engages in Intensive Preparations for Upcoming Quad Leaders' Summit
articles.explainer:
• India is actively involved in preparatory meetings for the next Quad Leaders' Summit, focusing on key agenda items.
• Discussions include enhancing cooperation on maritime security, critical and emerging technologies, and climate action in the Indo-Pacific.
• A major focus is on strengthening supply chain resilience and addressing infrastructure gaps in partner countries.
• India advocates for an inclusive and rules-based Indo-Pacific order, emphasizing non-coercive approaches.
• The summit aims to reinforce the Quad's commitment to regional stability and prosperity.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: Quad is a strategic dialogue between India, USA, Japan, and Australia.
Focuses on Indo-Pacific security, economy, and technology.
Aims for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
Addresses issues like maritime security, climate change, and supply chains.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's strategic objectives and priorities within the Quad framework, especially in the context of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the role of Quad in promoting maritime security, supply chain resilience, and cooperation on critical technologies.
Evaluate the criticisms leveled against Quad (e.g., being an 'Asian NATO') and how India addresses these concerns.
Examine the potential for Quad to evolve beyond a security dialogue into a broader platform for regional development and cooperation.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India, USA, Japan, and Australia displayed together, representing the Quad grouping.
articles.trivia: The idea of Quad was first mooted by Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe in 2007.

articles.headline: India Moves Towards Comprehensive AI Ethics Framework for Responsible Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a consultation paper on India's AI Ethics Framework.
• The framework aims to guide the responsible development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence across various sectors.
• Key principles include fairness, accountability, transparency, privacy, and security in AI systems.
• Emphasis is placed on preventing algorithmic bias and ensuring human-centric AI design.
• The initiative positions India as a leader in ethical AI governance, balancing innovation with societal well-being.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: India is developing an AI Ethics Framework.
Principles include fairness, accountability, transparency, privacy, and security.
Aims to prevent algorithmic bias and promote human-centric AI.
Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) is leading the initiative.
mainsPoints: Discuss the necessity of an AI Ethics Framework for India in the context of rapid AI development and adoption.
Analyze the key principles of India's proposed AI Ethics Framework and their importance in ensuring responsible AI.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing ethical AI guidelines, particularly concerning algorithmic bias and data privacy.
Suggest how India can balance fostering AI innovation with ensuring societal well-being and preventing potential harms.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic representing a human brain connected to digital circuits, symbolizing ethical AI development.
articles.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined by John McCarthy in 1956.

articles.headline: Indian Cities Adopt New Strategies to Mitigate Urban Heat Island Effect
articles.explainer:
• Major Indian metropolitan cities are implementing advanced strategies to combat the escalating Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect.
• Initiatives include promoting cool roofs, increasing green cover, implementing permeable pavements, and optimizing urban planning.
• The UHI effect leads to higher temperatures in urban areas compared to surrounding rural regions, exacerbating heatwaves.
• Municipal corporations are leveraging satellite data and climate models for targeted interventions and monitoring.
• These efforts are crucial for public health, energy efficiency, and climate resilience in rapidly urbanizing India.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect causes higher temperatures in cities.
Mitigation strategies include cool roofs, green spaces, and permeable pavements.
UHI exacerbates heatwaves and impacts public health.
Satellite data and climate models are used for planning.
mainsPoints: Explain the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect and its adverse consequences for Indian cities and their inhabitants.
Discuss the various strategies being adopted by urban local bodies to mitigate the UHI effect and enhance urban climate resilience.
Analyze the role of urban planning and governance in effectively addressing environmental challenges like UHI.
Suggest integrated approaches for sustainable urban development that incorporate climate change adaptation and mitigation measures.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a city showing green spaces and buildings with cool roofs, contrasting with traditional concrete structures.
articles.trivia: The Urban Heat Island effect can make city temperatures several degrees Celsius warmer than surrounding rural areas, especially at night.

articles.headline: Delimitation Commission Discussions Reignite Debate on Representation and Federalism
articles.explainer:
• Discussions around the potential formation of a new Delimitation Commission have reignited debates on parliamentary and assembly seat allocation.
• The upcoming delimitation, expected post-2026, will be based on the latest census data, potentially altering political representation.
• Southern states express concerns over reduced representation due to successful population control measures, while northern states may gain seats.
• The process involves redrawing constituency boundaries to ensure equal population representation, but raises federalism issues.
• Experts call for a balanced approach to ensure equitable representation while incentivizing population stabilization policies.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
prelimsPoints: Delimitation is the act of redrawing boundaries of Lok Sabha and state assembly constituencies.
Delimitation Commission is appointed by the President of India.
The next delimitation is expected after 2026, based on new census data.
Concerns exist regarding representation for states with lower population growth.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the implications of the upcoming delimitation exercise on India's federal structure and political representation.
Discuss the arguments made by states advocating for a freeze on delimitation based on the 1971 census and the counter-arguments.
Evaluate how the delimitation process can be made more equitable and transparent, addressing concerns of states that have successfully controlled population.
Examine the constitutional provisions governing delimitation and the role of the Delimitation Commission.
articles.imageDescription: A map of India with constituency boundaries being redrawn, symbolizing the delimitation process.
articles.trivia: The last major delimitation exercise in India was completed in 2008, based on the 2001 census data, but the number of seats remained frozen as per the 1971 census until 2026.

articles.headline: Republic Day Special: Revisiting the Architects and Philosophy of the Indian Constitution
articles.explainer:
• On Republic Day, the nation reflects on the monumental task of drafting and adopting the Indian Constitution.
• The Constituent Assembly, comprising diverse representatives, meticulously debated and shaped the foundational document.
• Key figures like Dr. B.R. Ambedkar, Jawaharlal Nehru, Sardar Patel, and others played pivotal roles in its formulation.
• The Constitution embodies ideals of justice, liberty, equality, and fraternity, establishing India as a sovereign, socialist, secular, democratic republic.
• Its enduring relevance lies in its adaptability and its role as the supreme law guiding India's governance and societal progress.
gsSubTopics: Indian Heritage and Culture, History and Geography of the World and Society
Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues
prelimsPoints: Indian Constitution was adopted on November 26, 1949, and came into effect on January 26, 1950.
Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee.
The Constituent Assembly took nearly 3 years to draft the Constitution.
Key ideals include justice, liberty, equality, and fraternity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and the significant challenges faced by the Constituent Assembly in drafting the Indian Constitution.
Analyze the contributions of key figures like Dr. B.R. Ambedkar and Jawaharlal Nehru in shaping the philosophical underpinnings of the Constitution.
Evaluate how the Preamble reflects the core ideals and aspirations of the Indian people and its enduring relevance.
Examine the concept of constitutionalism in India and how the Constitution has adapted to changing societal needs over time.
articles.imageDescription: A historical photograph of the Constituent Assembly members during a session, with Dr. B.R. Ambedkar in the foreground.
articles.trivia: The original copies of the Indian Constitution were handwritten in Hindi and English, and are preserved in a special helium-filled case in the Library of the Parliament of India.

articles.headline: India's Agricultural Exports Hit New Record Highs, Boost Rural Economy
articles.explainer:
• India's agricultural and processed food exports achieved unprecedented levels in the financial year 2025-26, setting new records.
• Key drivers include increased demand for Indian spices, rice, fruits, and vegetables in international markets.
• Government policies like the Agriculture Export Policy and various export promotion schemes have played a crucial role.
• The surge in exports has significantly boosted rural incomes and supported the livelihoods of millions of farmers.
• Focus on value addition, quality standards, and market diversification remains critical for sustaining this growth.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country
e-technology in the aid of farmers
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices
Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security
articles.source: Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority (APEDA)
prelimsPoints: India's agricultural exports reached record highs in FY 2025-26.
APEDA (Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority) promotes these exports.
Key products include spices, rice, fruits, and vegetables.
Government policies like Agriculture Export Policy support this growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the record growth in India's agricultural exports and its impact on the rural economy and farmers' incomes.
Discuss the role of government policies and export promotion schemes in enhancing India's competitiveness in global agricultural markets.
Evaluate the challenges faced by Indian agricultural exporters, including quality standards, supply chain logistics, and market access.
Suggest strategies for sustaining and further accelerating agricultural export growth, focusing on value addition and diversification.
articles.imageDescription: A vibrant display of various Indian agricultural products, including spices, rice, and fresh produce, ready for export.
articles.trivia: India is the world's largest producer of spices, pulses, milk, and jute, and the second-largest producer of rice, wheat, sugarcane, groundnut, and fruits and vegetables.

articles.headline: India Champions Global South Cooperation at G20 Ministerial, Emphasizes Climate Finance
articles.explainer:
• India, as a key voice for the Global South, actively participated in a G20 Ministerial meeting, pushing for enhanced cooperation.
• The discussions focused on fair and equitable climate finance mechanisms and technology transfer for developing nations.
• India highlighted the need for structural reforms in multilateral development banks to better serve the needs of the Global South.
• The agenda also included food and energy security, sustainable development goals, and digital public infrastructure sharing.
• This engagement reinforces India's leadership in advocating for the interests of developing countries on global platforms.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India represents the voice of the Global South in international forums like G20.
G20 Ministerial focused on climate finance and MDB reforms.
Other agenda items included food security, energy security, and SDGs.
India advocates for equitable global cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing role as a leader and advocate for the Global South in international forums like the G20.
Discuss the critical issues faced by the Global South, such as climate finance and sustainable development, and India's proposed solutions.
Evaluate the need for reforms in multilateral development banks to better address the challenges of developing countries.
Examine how India's promotion of Digital Public Infrastructure sharing can contribute to equitable development globally.
articles.imageDescription: A world map highlighting countries of the Global South, with India prominently featured, symbolizing its leadership.
articles.trivia: The term 'Global South' broadly refers to countries often characterized by lower income and higher vulnerability to climate change, primarily located in Asia, Africa, and Latin America.

articles.headline: New Archaeological Discoveries Shed Light on Ancient Indus Valley Civilization Trade Routes
articles.explainer:
• Recent archaeological excavations in Gujarat have unearthed new evidence suggesting previously unknown trade routes of the Indus Valley Civilization.
• Findings include unique pottery shards, seals, and artifacts indicating extensive maritime trade with West Asia.
• The discoveries provide deeper insights into the economic networks and cultural exchanges of one of the world's earliest civilizations.
• Researchers are employing advanced dating techniques and material analysis to establish precise timelines and origins.
• This sheds new light on the sophistication and reach of the Harappan civilization beyond its well-known urban centers.
gsSubTopics: Indian Heritage and Culture, History and Geography of the World and Society
Salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: New archaeological finds in Gujarat relate to Indus Valley Civilization trade.
Evidence suggests maritime trade with West Asia.
Indus Valley Civilization is one of the world's earliest urban cultures.
ASI (Archaeological Survey of India) is involved in excavations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in understanding the trade networks and cultural interactions of the Indus Valley Civilization.
Analyze how new evidence challenges or confirms existing theories about the extent and nature of Harappan civilization's foreign relations.
Evaluate the methods and technologies employed in modern archaeological excavations to reconstruct ancient civilizations.
Examine the contributions of the Indus Valley Civilization to human history and its relevance for understanding India's ancient heritage.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating a site, revealing ancient pottery and artifacts from the Indus Valley Civilization.
articles.trivia: The Indus Valley Civilization is also known as the Harappan Civilization, named after Harappa, the first site to be excavated.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission: Digital Health ID Adoption Sees Rapid Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) reported significant progress in the adoption of Digital Health IDs across India.
• Over 50 crore unique health IDs have been generated, facilitating seamless access to digital health records and services.
• The mission aims to create an integrated digital health infrastructure, connecting patients, doctors, and healthcare providers.
• Benefits include easier access to medical history, remote consultations, and improved healthcare delivery efficiency.
• Challenges remain in ensuring data privacy, digital literacy in rural areas, and interoperability across diverse health systems.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: ABDM aims to create India's integrated digital health infrastructure.
Digital Health ID (ABHA) is a unique 14-digit number.
Facilitates access to digital health records and services.
Implemented by the National Health Authority (NHA).
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in revolutionizing healthcare delivery in India.
Analyze the benefits and challenges associated with the widespread adoption of Digital Health IDs, particularly concerning data privacy and digital literacy.
Evaluate how an integrated digital health ecosystem can improve accessibility, efficiency, and quality of healthcare services.
Suggest measures to overcome implementation hurdles and ensure equitable access to digital health benefits across all sections of society.
articles.imageDescription: A hand holding a smartphone displaying a digital health ID, with a doctor and patient in the background.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY) is the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.

highlights: Nationwide protests erupt against UGC Bill 2026, raising concerns over education autonomy and access.
India celebrates 77th Republic Day, showcasing 'Nari Shakti' and indigenous defence, with French President as Chief Guest.
Economic Survey 2025-26 projects robust growth, highlighting green economy and Digital Public Infrastructure.
Supreme Court upholds core tenets of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, reinforcing right to privacy.
India accelerates border infrastructure development in the eastern sector amid evolving geopolitical dynamics.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the UGC Bill 2026, consider the following statements:
1. The Bill seeks to increase government funding for higher education while reducing private sector participation.
2. Protests against the Bill primarily concern the proposed centralisation of regulatory powers and potential commercialisation of education.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The explainer states 'Key contentious provisions include increased private sector participation, reduced government funding'. Statement 2 is correct as protests cite 'concerns over autonomy and access' and 'centralisation of regulatory powers' and 'commercialisation of higher education'.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements regarding India's 77th Republic Day celebrations (2026) is/are correct?
1. The theme of the parade emphasized 'Nari Shakti' and 'Viksit Bharat'.
2. The Prime Minister of France served as the Chief Guest for the event.
3. Indigenous defence capabilities were a prominent feature of the parade.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: 'The 77th Republic Day Parade on Kartavya Path highlighted 'Nari Shakti' (women power) and 'Viksit Bharat' by 2047'. Statement 2 is incorrect: 'French President Emmanuel Macron graced the occasion as the Chief Guest', not the Prime Minister. Statement 3 is correct: 'Indigenous defence capabilities were prominently displayed'.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following about the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023:
1. It aims to protect the fundamental right to privacy of individuals.
2. The Supreme Court has recently struck down its constitutional validity.
3. A 'data fiduciary' is an individual whose data is being processed, and a 'data principal' determines the purpose and means of processing personal data.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct, as the act emphasizes the fundamental right to privacy. Statement 2 is incorrect, as the Supreme Court 'largely upheld the constitutional validity' of the Act. Statement 3 reverses the definitions: a data principal is the individual whose data is processed, and a data fiduciary determines the purpose and means of processing.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy sources through electrolysis.
3. The mission's applications are limited to the transportation sector.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: 'The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export'. Statement 2 is correct: 'Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy sources via electrolysis'. Statement 3 is incorrect: 'Several pilot projects for green hydrogen applications in steel, refining, and transportation sectors have commenced operations', indicating broader applications.

prelimsMocks.question: The term 'Urban Heat Island' (UHI) effect, often seen in news, refers to:
options: (a) A phenomenon where coastal cities experience unusually low temperatures due to sea breezes.
(b) The process of land subsidence in urban areas due to excessive groundwater extraction.
(c) A metropolitan area that is significantly warmer than its surrounding rural areas due to human activities.
(d) The formation of dense fog and smog layers over cities during winter months.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) A metropolitan area that is significantly warmer than its surrounding rural areas due to human activities.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article states: 'The UHI effect leads to higher temperatures in urban areas compared to surrounding rural regions'. This is caused by factors like absorption of solar radiation by concrete and asphalt, lack of green spaces, and heat from vehicles and industries.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the potential impact of the proposed UGC Bill 2026 on the autonomy, accessibility, and quality of higher education in India. What measures can be adopted to balance reforms with the core principles of inclusive education?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors and Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the UGC Bill 2026 and the context of protests. Discuss concerns regarding reduced government funding, increased private participation, and centralisation impacting autonomy and accessibility. Analyze potential benefits like quality improvement and global competitiveness. Suggest a balanced approach involving robust regulatory frameworks, stakeholder consultations, ensuring affordability, and protecting academic freedom to achieve inclusive and quality education.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the strategic imperatives behind India's accelerated border infrastructure development, particularly in the eastern sector. How do these initiatives contribute to national security and what are the associated socio-economic implications for border populations?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Security challenges and their management in border areas; Linkages between development and spread of extremism. GS2: India and its neighbourhood- relations.
mainsMocks.approach: Explain the geopolitical context and security challenges in India's eastern borders. Detail the types of infrastructure being developed (roads, bridges, tunnels) and their role in enhancing troop mobility, logistics, and surveillance. Analyze the contribution to national security and deterrence. Discuss socio-economic benefits like improved connectivity, local employment, and integration, along with potential environmental or displacement challenges.

mainsMocks.question: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023 marks a significant step towards safeguarding the right to privacy in India. Analyze the key provisions of the Act and evaluate the balance it strikes between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests. What challenges remain in its effective implementation?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the DPDP Act 2023 and the Supreme Court's stance. Outline key provisions like data fiduciary obligations, data principal rights, consent mechanisms, and cross-border data transfer rules. Discuss how the Act attempts to balance privacy with state interests (e.g., national security exemptions). Identify implementation challenges such as digital literacy, ensuring data interoperability, capacity building for regulatory bodies, and preventing misuse of exemptions.

quickBytes.text: The University Grants Commission (UGC) was established in 1956 based on the recommendations of the Radhakrishnan Commission.
The first Republic Day Parade was held in 1950, and Kartavya Path (formerly Rajpath) has been its venue since 1955.
The Economic Survey is prepared by the Chief Economic Adviser (CEA) under the guidance of the Ministry of Finance.
France was the first country to sign a 'Strategic Partnership' with India in 1998, covering defence, space, and nuclear energy.
Shukrayaan-2 is ISRO's proposed mission to Venus, aiming to study its atmosphere and surface.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy, making it a zero-carbon fuel.
The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) of the Supreme Court declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right.
The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing and exports in key sectors.
The Quad is a strategic dialogue between India, USA, Japan, and Australia, focusing on the Indo-Pacific region.
The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) aims to create an integrated digital health infrastructure in India.

GS1.headline: Government Launches 'Saksham Nari' Initiative to Boost Women's Entrepreneurship and Digital Literacy
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched 'Saksham Nari', a pan-India initiative aimed at empowering women.
• The program focuses on promoting women's entrepreneurship through skill development, financial assistance, and market linkages.
• A significant component includes enhancing digital literacy among women in rural and semi-urban areas to bridge the digital divide.
• Partnerships with self-help groups (SHGs), NGOs, and private sector entities are central to the initiative's implementation.
• 'Saksham Nari' seeks to foster economic independence and social inclusion for women, aligning with 'Viksit Bharat' goals.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization
Population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues
Social empowerment
GS1.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: 'Saksham Nari' is a government initiative for women empowerment.
Focuses on entrepreneurship, skill development, and digital literacy.
Implemented by Ministry of Women and Child Development.
Aims to involve SHGs and private sector for market linkages.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted approach of the 'Saksham Nari' initiative in promoting women's empowerment and its potential impact on gender equality.
Analyze how enhancing digital literacy among women can bridge the digital divide and foster economic independence.
Evaluate the role of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and public-private partnerships in the successful implementation of such social welfare programs.
Suggest additional measures to further support women's entrepreneurship and ensure sustainable livelihoods for women in India.
GS1.imageDescription: A group of women in a rural setting attending a digital literacy workshop, using tablets.
GS1.trivia: The 'Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao' campaign, launched in 2015, aims to address the declining Child Sex Ratio and empower girls in India.

GS1.headline: Republic Day Special: Revisiting the Architects and Philosophy of the Indian Constitution
GS1.explainer:
• On Republic Day, the nation reflects on the monumental task of drafting and adopting the Indian Constitution.
• The Constituent Assembly, comprising diverse representatives, meticulously debated and shaped the foundational document.
• Key figures like Dr. B.R. Ambedkar, Jawaharlal Nehru, Sardar Patel, and others played pivotal roles in its formulation.
• The Constitution embodies ideals of justice, liberty, equality, and fraternity, establishing India as a sovereign, socialist, secular, democratic republic.
• Its enduring relevance lies in its adaptability and its role as the supreme law guiding India's governance and societal progress.
gsSubTopics: Indian Heritage and Culture, History and Geography of the World and Society
Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues
prelimsPoints: Indian Constitution was adopted on November 26, 1949, and came into effect on January 26, 1950.
Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee.
The Constituent Assembly took nearly 3 years to draft the Constitution.
Key ideals include justice, liberty, equality, and fraternity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and the significant challenges faced by the Constituent Assembly in drafting the Indian Constitution.
Analyze the contributions of key figures like Dr. B.R. Ambedkar and Jawaharlal Nehru in shaping the philosophical underpinnings of the Constitution.
Evaluate how the Preamble reflects the core ideals and aspirations of the Indian people and its enduring relevance.
Examine the concept of constitutionalism in India and how the Constitution has adapted to changing societal needs over time.
GS1.imageDescription: A historical photograph of the Constituent Assembly members during a session, with Dr. B.R. Ambedkar in the foreground.
GS1.trivia: The original copies of the Indian Constitution were handwritten in Hindi and English, and are preserved in a special helium-filled case in the Library of the Parliament of India.

GS1.headline: New Archaeological Discoveries Shed Light on Ancient Indus Valley Civilization Trade Routes
GS1.explainer:
• Recent archaeological excavations in Gujarat have unearthed new evidence suggesting previously unknown trade routes of the Indus Valley Civilization.
• Findings include unique pottery shards, seals, and artifacts indicating extensive maritime trade with West Asia.
• The discoveries provide deeper insights into the economic networks and cultural exchanges of one of the world's earliest civilizations.
• Researchers are employing advanced dating techniques and material analysis to establish precise timelines and origins.
• This sheds new light on the sophistication and reach of the Harappan civilization beyond its well-known urban centers.
gsSubTopics: Indian Heritage and Culture, History and Geography of the World and Society
Salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: New archaeological finds in Gujarat relate to Indus Valley Civilization trade.
Evidence suggests maritime trade with West Asia.
Indus Valley Civilization is one of the world's earliest urban cultures.
ASI (Archaeological Survey of India) is involved in excavations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in understanding the trade networks and cultural interactions of the Indus Valley Civilization.
Analyze how new evidence challenges or confirms existing theories about the extent and nature of Harappan civilization's foreign relations.
Evaluate the methods and technologies employed in modern archaeological excavations to reconstruct ancient civilizations.
Examine the contributions of the Indus Valley Civilization to human history and its relevance for understanding India's ancient heritage.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating a site, revealing ancient pottery and artifacts from the Indus Valley Civilization.
GS1.trivia: The Indus Valley Civilization is also known as the Harappan Civilization, named after Harappa, the first site to be excavated.

GS2.headline: Nationwide Protests Erupt Against UGC Bill 2026, Education Sector Demands Review
GS2.explainer:
• Students, faculty, and education activists across India staged massive protests against the proposed UGC Bill 2026, citing concerns over autonomy and access.
• Key contentious provisions include increased private sector participation, reduced government funding, and centralisation of regulatory powers.
• Critics argue the Bill could lead to commercialisation of higher education, making it inaccessible for economically weaker sections.
• The government defends the Bill, stating it aims to enhance quality, efficiency, and global competitiveness of Indian universities.
• Demonstrations were particularly strong in major educational hubs, with calls for a parliamentary review and wider stakeholder consultation.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: UGC Bill 2026 proposes major changes to higher education regulation.
Concerns include funding, private sector role, and institutional autonomy.
UGC (University Grants Commission) is a statutory body under Ministry of Education.
Protests highlight issues of access and equity in education.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the potential impact of the UGC Bill 2026 on the accessibility, quality, and equity of higher education in India.
Discuss the role of government in regulating higher education and balancing autonomy with accountability.
Evaluate the arguments for and against increased private sector involvement in education.
Suggest measures to ensure inclusive and quality education while pursuing reforms.
GS2.imageDescription: Students protesting with banners outside a university campus, demanding withdrawal of the UGC Bill 2026.
GS2.trivia: The University Grants Commission (UGC) was established in 1956, though its origins can be traced back to the recommendations of the Radhakrishnan Commission in 1948.

GS2.headline: India Showcases 'Nari Shakti' and Indigenous Defence Prowess at 77th Republic Day Parade
GS2.explainer:
• The 77th Republic Day Parade on Kartavya Path highlighted 'Nari Shakti' (women power) with all-women contingents and performances.
• Indigenous defence capabilities were prominently displayed, including advanced missile systems, tanks, and newly inducted drones.
• President Droupadi Murmu addressed the nation, emphasizing unity, progress, and the spirit of 'Viksit Bharat' by 2047.
• French President Emmanuel Macron graced the occasion as the Chief Guest, strengthening Indo-French strategic partnership.
• Cultural tableaux from various states showcased India's rich diversity and developmental initiatives, drawing widespread acclaim.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution
Significant provisions of the Indian Constitution
Government Policies and Interventions for Development
India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Republic Day is celebrated on January 26 to commemorate the adoption of the Constitution.
French President Emmanuel Macron was the Chief Guest for 2026.
Theme of the parade focused on 'Nari Shakti' and 'Viksit Bharat'.
Kartavya Path (formerly Rajpath) is the venue for the Republic Day Parade.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of Republic Day celebrations in reinforcing national identity and constitutional values.
Discuss how the emphasis on 'Nari Shakti' reflects India's progress in gender equality and women empowerment.
Evaluate the role of such national events in showcasing India's defence capabilities and promoting 'Make in India' initiatives.
Examine the geopolitical implications of inviting key foreign leaders as Republic Day Chief Guests, with specific reference to India-France relations.
GS2.imageDescription: An all-women contingent marching proudly down Kartavya Path during the Republic Day Parade.
GS2.trivia: The first Republic Day Parade was held in 1950 at Rajpath, which was renamed Kartavya Path in 2022.

GS2.headline: India-France Elevate Strategic Dialogue Post Republic Day, Focus on Defence & Space Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• Following the Republic Day celebrations, India and France held high-level strategic talks between NSA Ajit Doval and his French counterpart.
• Discussions centered on deepening defence cooperation, including technology transfer, joint production, and maritime security in the Indo-Pacific.
• Space collaboration, particularly in Earth observation and satellite navigation, emerged as a key area for future partnership.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to multilateralism and addressing global challenges like terrorism and climate change.
• The dialogue reinforced the 'Horizon 2047' roadmap for the bilateral relationship, aiming for enhanced strategic autonomy.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
India and its neighbourhood- relations
prelimsPoints: India and France are strategic partners, with cooperation in defence, space, and civil nuclear energy.
French President was Chief Guest at 2026 Republic Day.
Discussions focused on defence procurement, joint ventures, and space applications.
Horizon 2047 roadmap guides bilateral relations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving dynamics of India-France strategic partnership, particularly in the context of Indo-Pacific security.
Discuss the significance of defence technology transfer and joint production in enhancing India's strategic autonomy.
Evaluate the potential for expanded space cooperation between India and France and its implications for both nations.
Examine how bilateral ties contribute to addressing global challenges and promoting a multipolar world order.
GS2.imageDescription: National Security Advisors of India and France shaking hands during a high-level meeting.
GS2.trivia: France was the first country to sign a Strategic Partnership with India in 1998.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Core Tenets of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, Emphasizes Right to Privacy
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a significant ruling today, largely upholding the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023.
• The court emphasized the fundamental right to privacy as enshrined in Article 21 and its careful balance with legitimate state interests.
• Specific provisions related to data fiduciary obligations, data principal rights, and cross-border data transfers were clarified.
• The ruling provides much-needed legal clarity for technology companies and data processors operating in India.
• Critics' concerns regarding government exemptions and potential surveillance were partially addressed, with calls for strict oversight mechanisms.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: DPDP Act 2023 regulates personal data processing in India.
Supreme Court upheld its constitutional validity.
Right to Privacy is a fundamental right under Article 21.
The Act defines data fiduciary, data principal, and their rights/obligations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the Supreme Court's rationale in upholding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act and its implications for individual privacy in the digital age.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities presented by the DPDP Act for businesses and the government in managing personal data.
Evaluate the balance struck by the Act between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests, including national security.
Suggest mechanisms to ensure effective implementation and prevent misuse of exemptions under the DPDP Act.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal documents, symbolizing a Supreme Court judgment on data protection.
GS2.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) by the Supreme Court declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

GS2.headline: India Engages in Intensive Preparations for Upcoming Quad Leaders' Summit
GS2.explainer:
• India is actively involved in preparatory meetings for the next Quad Leaders' Summit, focusing on key agenda items.
• Discussions include enhancing cooperation on maritime security, critical and emerging technologies, and climate action in the Indo-Pacific.
• A major focus is on strengthening supply chain resilience and addressing infrastructure gaps in partner countries.
• India advocates for an inclusive and rules-based Indo-Pacific order, emphasizing non-coercive approaches.
• The summit aims to reinforce the Quad's commitment to regional stability and prosperity.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: Quad is a strategic dialogue between India, USA, Japan, and Australia.
Focuses on Indo-Pacific security, economy, and technology.
Aims for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
Addresses issues like maritime security, climate change, and supply chains.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's strategic objectives and priorities within the Quad framework, especially in the context of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the role of Quad in promoting maritime security, supply chain resilience, and cooperation on critical technologies.
Evaluate the criticisms leveled against Quad (e.g., being an 'Asian NATO') and how India addresses these concerns.
Examine the potential for Quad to evolve beyond a security dialogue into a broader platform for regional development and cooperation.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India, USA, Japan, and Australia displayed together, representing the Quad grouping.
GS2.trivia: The idea of Quad was first mooted by Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe in 2007.

GS2.headline: Delimitation Commission Discussions Reignite Debate on Representation and Federalism
GS2.explainer:
• Discussions around the potential formation of a new Delimitation Commission have reignited debates on parliamentary and assembly seat allocation.
• The upcoming delimitation, expected post-2026, will be based on the latest census data, potentially altering political representation.
• Southern states express concerns over reduced representation due to successful population control measures, while northern states may gain seats.
• The process involves redrawing constituency boundaries to ensure equal population representation, but raises federalism issues.
• Experts call for a balanced approach to ensure equitable representation while incentivizing population stabilization policies.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
prelimsPoints: Delimitation is the act of redrawing boundaries of Lok Sabha and state assembly constituencies.
Delimitation Commission is appointed by the President of India.
The next delimitation is expected after 2026, based on new census data.
Concerns exist regarding representation for states with lower population growth.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the implications of the upcoming delimitation exercise on India's federal structure and political representation.
Discuss the arguments made by states advocating for a freeze on delimitation based on the 1971 census and the counter-arguments.
Evaluate how the delimitation process can be made more equitable and transparent, addressing concerns of states that have successfully controlled population.
Examine the constitutional provisions governing delimitation and the role of the Delimitation Commission.
GS2.imageDescription: A map of India with constituency boundaries being redrawn, symbolizing the delimitation process.
GS2.trivia: The last major delimitation exercise in India was completed in 2008, based on the 2001 census data, but the number of seats remained frozen as per the 1971 census until 2026.

GS2.headline: India Champions Global South Cooperation at G20 Ministerial, Emphasizes Climate Finance
GS2.explainer:
• India, as a key voice for the Global South, actively participated in a G20 Ministerial meeting, pushing for enhanced cooperation.
• The discussions focused on fair and equitable climate finance mechanisms and technology transfer for developing nations.
• India highlighted the need for structural reforms in multilateral development banks to better serve the needs of the Global South.
• The agenda also included food and energy security, sustainable development goals, and digital public infrastructure sharing.
• This engagement reinforces India's leadership in advocating for the interests of developing countries on global platforms.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India represents the voice of the Global South in international forums like G20.
G20 Ministerial focused on climate finance and MDB reforms.
Other agenda items included food security, energy security, and SDGs.
India advocates for equitable global cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing role as a leader and advocate for the Global South in international forums like the G20.
Discuss the critical issues faced by the Global South, such as climate finance and sustainable development, and India's proposed solutions.
Evaluate the need for reforms in multilateral development banks to better address the challenges of developing countries.
Examine how India's promotion of Digital Public Infrastructure sharing can contribute to equitable development globally.
GS2.imageDescription: A world map highlighting countries of the Global South, with India prominently featured, symbolizing its leadership.
GS2.trivia: The term 'Global South' broadly refers to countries often characterized by lower income and higher vulnerability to climate change, primarily located in Asia, Africa, and Latin America.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission: Digital Health ID Adoption Sees Rapid Growth
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) reported significant progress in the adoption of Digital Health IDs across India.
• Over 50 crore unique health IDs have been generated, facilitating seamless access to digital health records and services.
• The mission aims to create an integrated digital health infrastructure, connecting patients, doctors, and healthcare providers.
• Benefits include easier access to medical history, remote consultations, and improved healthcare delivery efficiency.
• Challenges remain in ensuring data privacy, digital literacy in rural areas, and interoperability across diverse health systems.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: ABDM aims to create India's integrated digital health infrastructure.
Digital Health ID (ABHA) is a unique 14-digit number.
Facilitates access to digital health records and services.
Implemented by the National Health Authority (NHA).
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in revolutionizing healthcare delivery in India.
Analyze the benefits and challenges associated with the widespread adoption of Digital Health IDs, particularly concerning data privacy and digital literacy.
Evaluate how an integrated digital health ecosystem can improve accessibility, efficiency, and quality of healthcare services.
Suggest measures to overcome implementation hurdles and ensure equitable access to digital health benefits across all sections of society.
GS2.imageDescription: A hand holding a smartphone displaying a digital health ID, with a doctor and patient in the background.
GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY) is the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.

GS3.headline: Economic Survey 2025-26 Hints at Robust Growth, Focus on Green Economy & Digital Public Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• The Economic Survey 2025-26, released ahead of the Union Budget, projects India's economy to maintain a strong growth trajectory.
• It emphasizes the crucial role of green economy initiatives and Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in driving sustainable development.
• The survey highlights continued capital expenditure by the government and private sector investment as key growth drivers.
• Challenges identified include global supply chain disruptions, inflationary pressures, and the need for skill development.
• Recommendations include further reforms in agriculture, labour, and financial sectors to unlock full potential.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Government Budgeting
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Economic Survey is released annually by the Ministry of Finance before the Union Budget.
It provides a detailed review of the economy's performance.
Key focus areas for 2025-26 survey include green economy and DPI.
Projections for GDP growth are usually provided.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key findings of the Economic Survey 2025-26 regarding India's growth prospects and challenges.
Discuss the significance of 'Green Economy' and 'Digital Public Infrastructure' in achieving sustainable and inclusive growth.
Evaluate the government's strategy of increasing capital expenditure to stimulate economic activity.
Suggest policy measures to address the identified challenges of inflation and global supply chain disruptions.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic illustrating India's projected economic growth trajectory with pillars representing green economy and digital infrastructure.
GS3.trivia: The first Economic Survey of India was presented in 1950-51, and until 1964, it was presented along with the Union Budget.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Economic Survey and Key Economic Indicators

GS3.headline: ISRO's Shukrayaan-2 Mission Gears Up for Launch, Focus on Venusian Atmosphere & Surface Mapping
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) provided an update on the Shukrayaan-2 mission, confirming its readiness for an upcoming launch window.
• The mission aims to study the Venusian atmosphere, its composition, and potential geological activity using advanced instruments.
• Key objectives include high-resolution mapping of Venus's surface and subsurface features, utilizing synthetic aperture radar (SAR).
• Shukrayaan-2 builds upon global efforts to understand Venus, often called Earth's 'evil twin' due to its extreme conditions.
• The mission underscores India's growing capabilities in interplanetary exploration and scientific research.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Shukrayaan-2 is ISRO's proposed mission to Venus.
Objectives include studying Venus's atmosphere and surface.
Utilizes Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) for mapping.
It is part of India's interplanetary exploration program.
mainsPoints: Discuss the scientific objectives and potential contributions of ISRO's Shukrayaan-2 mission to planetary science.
Evaluate India's growing capabilities in space exploration and its implications for global scientific collaboration.
Examine the technological challenges involved in missions to Venus and how ISRO is addressing them.
Highlight the significance of such missions in inspiring scientific temper and technological innovation within the country.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of the Shukrayaan-2 orbiter approaching Venus, with scientific instruments deployed.
GS3.trivia: Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system, despite being farther from the Sun than Mercury, due to its dense atmosphere causing a runaway greenhouse effect.

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Achieves Key Milestones, Boosts Renewable Energy Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission announced significant progress in establishing green hydrogen production facilities and infrastructure.
• Several pilot projects for green hydrogen applications in steel, refining, and transportation sectors have commenced operations.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Government incentives and policy support are attracting substantial private investment into the renewable energy sector.
• This advancement is crucial for India's climate commitments and achieving energy independence by 2047.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to establish India as a global green hydrogen hub.
Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy sources via electrolysis.
Applications include steel, refining, and transportation.
It is crucial for India's climate goals and energy security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of transitioning to a green hydrogen-based economy.
Examine the challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and infrastructure in India.
Suggest policy interventions and technological advancements required to accelerate the adoption of green hydrogen across sectors.
GS3.imageDescription: An industrial facility with electrolyzers producing green hydrogen, powered by wind turbines in the background.
GS3.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, making it a zero-carbon fuel.

GS3.headline: India Accelerates Border Infrastructure Development in Eastern Sector Amid Geopolitical Shifts
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced accelerated projects for road, bridge, and tunnel construction along India's eastern borders.
• The push for infrastructure development aims to enhance troop mobility, logistics, and surveillance capabilities in strategically critical regions.
• Modernization includes advanced surveillance technologies and integration with local communities for intelligence gathering.
• The move is seen as a response to evolving geopolitical dynamics and security challenges in the Himalayan frontiers.
• Local employment generation and improved connectivity for border populations are additional benefits cited by officials.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
prelimsPoints: India is accelerating infrastructure development on its eastern borders.
Projects include roads, bridges, and tunnels.
Aims to enhance troop mobility and surveillance.
Response to geopolitical shifts and security challenges.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic imperatives behind India's accelerated border infrastructure development in the eastern sector.
Analyze how enhanced connectivity and surveillance capabilities contribute to national security and border management.
